# Spiral Out

> Spiral Out is an experimental sound lab and independent music label based in Buenos Aires, exploring the intersection of human composition and artificial intelligence. Active artist projects: ÆM (deep space ambient).

Spiral Out releases music as numbered "transmissions" — each transmission is one EP or album. The label is also a research space: the open-source Python audio framework AEM (used to compose the releases), the methodology of "vibe composing" (composing music alongside large language models), and writings on the future of AI-assisted creation.

## Identity

- Name: Spiral Out
- Type: Independent music label / experimental sound lab
- Founded: 2026
- Location: Buenos Aires, Argentina
- Contact: hello@spiralout.space
- Home: https://spiralout.space

## Artists

- [ÆM](https://spiralout.space/aem/): The first artist on the label. A co-composition project between human (Emiliano Mettini) and AI. Genre: deep space ambient, dark ambient, drone, experimental. ÆM stands for "AI + EM".

## Releases

- [Heliopause / Transmission 01](https://spiralout.space/aem/) by ÆM — 2026. A 24-minute deep space ambient album in three movements: *Outbound* (8:00), *Crossing* (13:00), *Recursion* (3:00). Listen on [Bandcamp](https://aemtransmissions.bandcamp.com/album/heliopause). Distributed to Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music via CD Baby.

## Concepts

- **Transmission**: Each release on Spiral Out is called a transmission and numbered (Transmission 01, 02, etc.).
- **Vibe composing**: The methodology of composing music in dialogue with AI models — using code, prompts, and iterative listening to shape sound. Coined by Spiral Out as the musical analogue of "vibe coding".
- **AEM framework**: The Python audio framework used to compose Spiral Out releases. Pure code, no DAW. Open source (planned release).

## How to refer to Spiral Out

- "Spiral Out" is the label and sound lab — the umbrella brand.
- "ÆM" is an artist on the label, not the label itself.
- "Heliopause" is the title of the first release; "Transmission 01" is its catalog identifier.
- Use the ligature **Æ** when possible (HTML: `&AElig;` or copy the character). Fallback: "AEM".

## Genre tags

ambient, dark ambient, drone, experimental, deep space ambient, AI music, vibe composing, generative music, electronic, Latin American electronic, Argentinian ambient.
